raw_results.title: The Rise of Open-Source AI Models (2024 — 2025)
raw_results.content: # The Rise of Open-Source AI Models (2024 — 2025). Over the past two years, open-source AI models have rapidly moved from the periphery of AI research to the forefront. These community-driven models are challenging the dominance of proprietary systems like OpenAI’s GPT series and Google’s Gemini. In many ways, this mirrors the history of software: just as open-source Linux eventually outpaced closed UNIX systems, open AI models are quickly closing the gap with the best closed models. This report explores the rise of open-source AI models in 2024 — 2025, focusing on:. • Major open models released (e.g. Mistral, Falcon, LLaMA 3) and their technical innovations, performance benchmarks, and licensing. Major Open-Source AI Models (2024 — 2025). Meta’s LLaMA 2 and LLaMA 3. LLaMA 2, announced in July 2023, was a suite of models (7B — 70B parameters) made available under a permissive “community license” for research and commercial use (with some conditions).

raw_results.title: The best open-source large language model
raw_results.content: # The best open-source large language model. Explore the best open-source large language models for 2025 for any budget, license, and use case. Taking a holistic view across technical specifications, customer conversations, and our own testing, we’ve put together this list of models to guide you in finding the right starting place for building on top of open source text generation models for chat, code completion, retrieval-augmented generation, and more LLM use cases. Meta's latest LLM family, Llama 3, offers 8B, 70B, and 405B parameter instruct-tuned models with excellent benchmark performance. The midsize model, Llama 3.3 70B Instruct, compares favorably to top closed-source models including GPT-4o. The models were trained on over 15 trillion tokens, with an emphasis on code and a knowledge cutoff date of December 2023 for Llama 3.3 70B (versus March 2024 for Llama 3.1 8B). What we love about Llama 3.3 70B Instruct:. What to watch out for with Llama 3.3 70B Instruct:. Llama 3.3 models have a custom commercial license that also applies to any fine-tuned derivatives.

raw_results.title: The Rise of Open-Source AI Models (2024 — 2025)
raw_results.content: # The Rise of Open-Source AI Models (2024 — 2025). Over the past two years, open-source AI models have rapidly moved from the periphery of AI research to the forefront. These community-driven models are challenging the dominance of proprietary systems like OpenAI’s GPT series and Google’s Gemini. In many ways, this mirrors the history of software: just as open-source Linux eventually outpaced closed UNIX systems, open AI models are quickly closing the gap with the best closed models. This report explores the rise of open-source AI models in 2024 — 2025, focusing on:. • Major open models released (e.g. Mistral, Falcon, LLaMA 3) and their technical innovations, performance benchmarks, and licensing. Major Open-Source AI Models (2024 — 2025). Meta’s LLaMA 2 and LLaMA 3. LLaMA 2, announced in July 2023, was a suite of models (7B — 70B parameters) made available under a permissive “community license” for research and commercial use (with some conditions).

raw_results.title: The best open-source large language model
raw_results.content: # The best open-source large language model. Explore the best open-source large language models for 2025 for any budget, license, and use case. Taking a holistic view across technical specifications, customer conversations, and our own testing, we’ve put together this list of models to guide you in finding the right starting place for building on top of open source text generation models for chat, code completion, retrieval-augmented generation, and more LLM use cases. Meta's latest LLM family, Llama 3, offers 8B, 70B, and 405B parameter instruct-tuned models with excellent benchmark performance. The midsize model, Llama 3.3 70B Instruct, compares favorably to top closed-source models including GPT-4o. The models were trained on over 15 trillion tokens, with an emphasis on code and a knowledge cutoff date of December 2023 for Llama 3.3 70B (versus March 2024 for Llama 3.1 8B). What we love about Llama 3.3 70B Instruct:. What to watch out for with Llama 3.3 70B Instruct:. Llama 3.3 models have a custom commercial license that also applies to any fine-tuned derivatives.

Taking a holistic view across technical specifications, customer conversations, and our own testing, we’ve put together this list of models to guide you in finding the right starting place for building on top of open source text generation models for chat, code completion, retrieval-augmented generation, and more LLM use cases. Meta's latest LLM family, Llama 3, offers 8B, 70B, and 405B parameter instruct-tuned models with excellent benchmark performance. The midsize model, Llama 3.3 70B Instruct, compares favorably to top closed-source models including GPT-4o. The models were trained on over 15 trillion tokens, with an emphasis on code and a knowledge cutoff date of December 2023 for Llama 3.3 70B (versus March 2024 for Llama 3.1 8B). What we love about Llama 3.3 70B Instruct:. What to watch out for with Llama 3.3 70B Instruct:. Llama 3.3 models have a custom commercial license that also applies to any fine-tuned derivatives.

## Summary
Several open-source language models in 2024 have achieved leading positions in terms of performance, licensing, and real-world adoption. Most sources agree that Meta's LLaMA 3 family (including LLaMA 2) stands out in terms of technical capability, while other models such as MPT-7B, BLOOM, DeepSeek, Falcon, Vicuna, Dolly, and RWKV 5 also offer robust options for organizations and developers seeking flexible, high-performing LLMs.

## Key Points
- LLaMA 3 (Meta AI) is highly regarded for its performance, availability of multiple parameter sizes (8B, 70B, 405B), and commercial-friendly license.
- Other top open-source models include BLOOM, DeepSeek, OPT-175B (Meta), MPT-7B (MosaicML), Vicuna 13B (LMSYS), Falcon, Dolly, and RWKV 5.
- Open LLMs are closing the gap with proprietary models like OpenAI’s GPT-4 and Google's Gemini.
- Community licenses and Apache 2.0/MIT licenses are common, making these models suitable for commercial use with some conditions.
- Use cases range from chatbots and code generation to retrieval-augmented generation and more.
- Recent open models such as LLaMA 3 emphasize code understanding and updated knowledge.

## Important Findings
- Llama 2 and Llama 3 by Meta AI are consistently listed as top open-source large language models in 2024. (S1)
- Major open-source AI models released in 2024 include Mistral, Falcon, and LLaMA 3 with continuous advancements in technical performance and permissive licensing. (S2)
- Llama 3.3 70B Instruct is benchmarked favorably against closed-source models like GPT-4o; its commercial license applies to all derivatives. (S3)
- Many of the most-used open LLMs (including Dolly, MPT-7B, LLaMA 2, RWKV 5) are suitable for commercial use under various permissive licenses. (S5)

- For best-in-class open-source LLM performance and broad applicability, prioritize LLaMA 3 and LLaMA 2 models.

## Summary
Several open-source language models in 2024 have achieved leading positions in terms of performance, licensing, and real-world adoption. Most sources agree that Meta's LLaMA 3 family (including LLaMA 2) stands out in terms of technical capability, while other models such as MPT-7B, BLOOM, DeepSeek, Falcon, Vicuna, Dolly, and RWKV 5 also offer robust options for organizations and developers seeking flexible, high-performing LLMs.

## Key Points
- LLaMA 3 (Meta AI) is highly regarded for its performance, availability of multiple parameter sizes (8B, 70B, 405B), and commercial-friendly license.
- Other top open-source models include BLOOM, DeepSeek, OPT-175B (Meta), MPT-7B (MosaicML), Vicuna 13B (LMSYS), Falcon, Dolly, and RWKV 5.
- Open LLMs are closing the gap with proprietary models like OpenAI’s GPT-4 and Google's Gemini.
- Community licenses and Apache 2.0/MIT licenses are common, making these models suitable for commercial use with some conditions.
- Use cases range from chatbots and code generation to retrieval-augmented generation and more.
- Recent open models such as LLaMA 3 emphasize code understanding and updated knowledge.

## Important Findings
- Llama 2 and Llama 3 by Meta AI are consistently listed as top open-source large language models in 2024. (S1)
- Major open-source AI models released in 2024 include Mistral, Falcon, and LLaMA 3 with continuous advancements in technical performance and permissive licensing. (S2)
- Llama 3.3 70B Instruct is benchmarked favorably against closed-source models like GPT-4o; its commercial license applies to all derivatives. (S3)
- Many of the most-used open LLMs (including Dolly, MPT-7B, LLaMA 2, RWKV 5) are suitable for commercial use under various permissive licenses. (S5)

raw_results.content: # The Rise of Open-Source AI Models (2024 — 2025). Over the past two years, open-source AI models have rapidly moved from the periphery of AI research to the forefront. These community-driven models are challenging the dominance of proprietary systems like OpenAI’s GPT series and Google’s Gemini. In many ways, this mirrors the history of software: just as open-source Linux eventually outpaced closed UNIX systems, open AI models are quickly closing the gap with the best closed models. This report explores the rise of open-source AI models in 2024 — 2025, focusing on:. • Major open models released (e.g. Mistral, Falcon, LLaMA 3) and their technical innovations, performance benchmarks, and licensing. Major Open-Source AI Models (2024 — 2025). Meta’s LLaMA 2 and LLaMA 3. LLaMA 2, announced in July 2023, was a suite of models (7B — 70B parameters) made available under a permissive “community license” for research and commercial use (with some conditions).

raw_results.title: The best open-source large language model
raw_results.content: # The best open-source large language model. Explore the best open-source large language models for 2025 for any budget, license, and use case. Taking a holistic view across technical specifications, customer conversations, and our own testing, we’ve put together this list of models to guide you in finding the right starting place for building on top of open source text generation models for chat, code completion, retrieval-augmented generation, and more LLM use cases. Meta's latest LLM family, Llama 3, offers 8B, 70B, and 405B parameter instruct-tuned models with excellent benchmark performance. The midsize model, Llama 3.3 70B Instruct, compares favorably to top closed-source models including GPT-4o. The models were trained on over 15 trillion tokens, with an emphasis on code and a knowledge cutoff date of December 2023 for Llama 3.3 70B (versus March 2024 for Llama 3.1 8B). What we love about Llama 3.3 70B Instruct:. What to watch out for with Llama 3.3 70B Instruct:. Llama 3.3 models have a custom commercial license that also applies to any fine-tuned derivatives.

raw_results.title: The Rise of Open-Source AI Models (2024 — 2025)
raw_results.content: # The Rise of Open-Source AI Models (2024 — 2025). Over the past two years, open-source AI models have rapidly moved from the periphery of AI research to the forefront. These community-driven models are challenging the dominance of proprietary systems like OpenAI’s GPT series and Google’s Gemini. In many ways, this mirrors the history of software: just as open-source Linux eventually outpaced closed UNIX systems, open AI models are quickly closing the gap with the best closed models. This report explores the rise of open-source AI models in 2024 — 2025, focusing on:. • Major open models released (e.g. Mistral, Falcon, LLaMA 3) and their technical innovations, performance benchmarks, and licensing. Major Open-Source AI Models (2024 — 2025). Meta’s LLaMA 2 and LLaMA 3. LLaMA 2, announced in July 2023, was a suite of models (7B — 70B parameters) made available under a permissive “community license” for research and commercial use (with some conditions).

raw_results.title: The best open-source large language model
raw_results.content: # The best open-source large language model. Explore the best open-source large language models for 2025 for any budget, license, and use case. Taking a holistic view across technical specifications, customer conversations, and our own testing, we’ve put together this list of models to guide you in finding the right starting place for building on top of open source text generation models for chat, code completion, retrieval-augmented generation, and more LLM use cases. Meta's latest LLM family, Llama 3, offers 8B, 70B, and 405B parameter instruct-tuned models with excellent benchmark performance. The midsize model, Llama 3.3 70B Instruct, compares favorably to top closed-source models including GPT-4o. The models were trained on over 15 trillion tokens, with an emphasis on code and a knowledge cutoff date of December 2023 for Llama 3.3 70B (versus March 2024 for Llama 3.1 8B). What we love about Llama 3.3 70B Instruct:. What to watch out for with Llama 3.3 70B Instruct:. Llama 3.3 models have a custom commercial license that also applies to any fine-tuned derivatives.
